title: Liparis androchilus
intro: Liparis androchilus, also known as the "Coral-root", is a type of orchid native to North America, characterized by its unique, subterranean habit and distinctive coral-like roots. Traditionally, Liparis androchilus has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Liparis androchilus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Liparis androchilus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Liparis androchilus is a species of orchid found in tropical Asia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Liparis. The classification of Liparis androchilus is further divided into the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Malaxideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup. The exact classification of Liparis androchilus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Orchidoideae, Tribe: Malaxideae, Genus: Liparis, Species: L. androchilus.
